name: Right Radius
description: Lateral bone of the forearm. Its head articulates with the capitulum of the humerus; distally it forms the major articulation of the wrist.
articulations: Capitulum of the humerus — elbow joint
Radial notch of the ulna (proximal) and ulnar notch (distal) — radioulnar joints
Scaphoid and lunate carpal bones — wrist (radiocarpal) joint
clinical: Colles' fracture: a distal radius fracture with dorsal displacement, typically from a fall on an outstretched hand.
Essex-Lopresti injury: radial head fracture with distal radioulnar joint dislocation.
